issues.description: The sync-back effect (lines 20-22) sets `localValue` to `query` unconditionally on every `query` change, including the change triggered by the debounce effect itself. This creates a tight loop: user types → debounce fires → URL updates (query changes) → sync-back effect fires → setLocalValue(query) overwrites the current input mid-typing. The other two debounce components (ProductFilters.tsx and UsersPageContent.tsx) guard this with an equality check (`if (q !== searchInput)`), but SearchBar.tsx does not.

issues.category: Memory Leak / Missing cancelAnimationFrame on early return
issues.description: `animId` is initialized to `0` and only set when `document.fonts.ready` resolves. The cleanup function captures `animId` via closure; however, `animId` is a local `let` variable inside the effect, so the cleanup always reads its latest value correctly. This is fine. However, there is a subtle issue: if `document.fonts.ready` resolves AFTER the component unmounts (i.e., `cancelled = true`), the `.then()` callback returns early without scheduling any `requestAnimationFrame`, but the cleanup has already run with `animId === 0`, calling `cancelAnimationFrame(0)` which is a no-op. This is safe in practice. The actual concern is in the `.catch()` path on line 180: when fonts fail to load, `requestAnimationFrame(draw)` is called even though `cancelled` might be true at that point — the `!cancelled` check is correct there. No actionable bug, but the `animId = 0` initial value means if the component unmounts before fonts load, `cancelAnimationFrame(0)` is called unnecessarily (harmless). DOWNGRADED to info.
issues.fix: This is not a real bug — the `cancelled` flag prevents any frame from being scheduled after unmount. No fix required. Downgraded to info.

issues.category: Null/Undefined Access — Initial State from URL Param
issues.description: `params.search` has a default of `""` (parseAsString.withDefault("")), so it cannot be null at runtime. However, the initial `useState(params.search)` call captures the value at mount time. If this component is server-rendered and hydrated, the initial URL params may differ between server and client, causing a hydration mismatch where `filterInput` is initialized with the server-side value. The sync-back `useEffect` fixes this post-mount, but there will be one render where `filterInput` does not match the actual URL state. This is a known pattern limitation, not a critical bug, but it can cause a visual flicker or one extra debounce timer.
issues.fix: This is an accepted trade-off with nuqs + local controlled input. The sync-back guard pattern already in place (lines 32-38) mitigates it. No immediate action required — track as known limitation.

issues.category: Stale Closure in useEffect — FeaturedRibbon foreground color
issues.description: `getComputedStyle(canvas)` is called inside the `useEffect` body (runs once per dep change), not inside the animation loop. This means `foregroundColor` and `bgColor` are captured at effect-run time. If the user switches to dark mode after the component mounts, the canvas continues using the old colors until the effect re-runs (which it won't, because none of its deps — `s`, `baseFontSize`, `textOffset`, `accentVar`, `label` — change on theme switch). The ribbon will display wrong colors until remount or prop change.

The `handleSearch` callback catches all errors from `search(value)` and silently sets results to `[]`. This means network errors, auth errors, or unexpected exceptions during delegate search are swallowed without any user-facing feedback. The user sees an empty list and has no indication that an error occurred.

patterns.description: Consistent debounce-with-cleanup pattern: all debounce timers (SearchBar, ProductFilters, UsersPageContent, useAutoSavePaymentMethod) are cleaned up via useEffect return. This prevents timer leaks on unmount.

Object URL lifecycle is consistently managed with ref+state double-tracking pattern (ReceiptUpload, ResubmitEvidenceForm): the previewUrlRef keeps the latest URL for unmount cleanup while state drives rendering. Both components correctly revoke old URLs before creating new ones.
All setInterval usages (ExpirationLabel, ReceivedOrderCard) include proper clearInterval cleanup in the useEffect return. No memory leaks from interval timers.

The checkout order route (POST /api/checkout/orders) performs proper server-side stock validation and price calculation, preventing client-side price/quantity manipulation. The buyer_submission validation is thorough with UUID regex checks.

SearchBar.tsx missing equality guard in sync-back useEffect — inconsistent with the identical pattern in ProductFilters.tsx and UsersPageContent.tsx which both guard with `if (q !== searchInput)` / `if (urlValue !== filterInput)`. This asymmetry suggests SearchBar was not updated when the guard was added to the other components.
patterns.rule_suggestion: Consider adding a lint rule or shared hook (e.g., `useUrlSyncedInput`) to enforce the equality-guard pattern for all debounce→URL→sync-back flows.
